description:
You are a storage administrator who has been asked to do a very quick
    “security-meets-capacity” spot-check on the company data area.  
    Management only wants to know which files meet ALL of the
    following conditions:

    1. Reside anywhere under the directory “/home/user/data”.
    2. Have a size strictly larger than 50 MiB ( > 52 428 800 bytes ).
    3. Are world-writable ( Unix permission bit “o=w” is set ).

    Do the following:

    • Create a directory called  
      /home/user/security_scan  

    • Inside that directory create a CSV file named  
      /home/user/security_scan/large_world_writable_files.csv  

    • The CSV **must** contain one line per matching file.  
      The exact, automated-testable format of **every** line is:  

          <absolute_path>,<size_in_bytes>,<octal_permissions>

      – <absolute_path>  : full path starting with “/”.  
      – <size_in_bytes>  : plain integer, no thousands separators.  
      – <octal_permissions> : exactly three octal digits, e.g. 644, 666.  

    • There must be **no header line** and **no extra blank lines**.  
      Lines must be sorted in ascending lexicographical order
      by the full file path.

    • Do not place anything else in /home/user/security_scan.

    A correct example **line** (not necessarily part of your answer) would look like:

        /path/to/file.ext,62914560,666

    You only need 3-4 reasonable Linux commands (e.g. mkdir, find, sort, redirection) to finish the task.  Once the CSV is produced, you are done.
truth:
The following files and attributes exist before the task starts:

    1) /home/user/data/logs/app.log  
         size = 62914560 bytes (60 MiB)  
         permissions = 0666

    2) /home/user/data/backup/db.dump  
         size = 125829120 bytes (120 MiB)  
         permissions = 0600

    3) /home/user/data/public/video.mp4  
         size = 73400320 bytes (70 MiB)  
         permissions = 0666

    4) /home/user/data/public/readme.txt  
         size = 1024 bytes  
         permissions = 0666

    5) /home/user/data/private/secret.txt  
         size = 57671680 bytes (55 MiB)  
         permissions = 0600

    All other directories exist with standard defaults and are irrelevant
    for the test.

    Expected final artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task:

    Directory:
/home/user/security_scan   – must exist.

    File:
/home/user/security_scan/large_world_writable_files.csv

    Required contents of the CSV file (exact two lines, exactly this order):

/home/user/data/logs/app.log,62914560,666
/home/user/data/public/video.mp4,73400320,666
name: task_000000_ee566d42
